offerings.label: Product leadership, at every depth
offerings.heading: Three ways to put senior product leadership on your team.

items.title: Fractional CPO
items.subtitle: Full product leadership, scoped to your inflection point.
items.body: Own strategy, align go-to-market, and drive execution — without the cost or ramp of a full-time hire. Embedded in your team, operating on your cadence.
triggers: Founder-led product — ready to hand it to a seasoned operator

items.body: Board-level guidance, product strategy definition and review, critical decision support and revising Ways of Working. High-leverage access when it matters most.

items.label: ARR growth at Monday.com, scaled past $1B ARR

about.label: Who I am
about.heading: Meet Guy Assedou.
about.subtitle: The operator behind the practice.
about.quote: What drives me is helping leaders realize their vision and build products that create real impact.

points.title: The inflection point is the job
points.body: The most interesting moments in a company's life happen at transition — when there's already a foundation, but the path to the next phase isn't clear yet. The challenge is rarely a lack of ideas. It's knowing where to focus, what to build now, and how to make the daily work move the metrics that actually matter.

points.title: Pattern recognition, earned
points.body: I've built products across B2B SaaS, B2C2C, and consumer — global enterprises and early-stage startups, horizontal platforms and vertical markets. Operating across enough distinct contexts is what turns experience into instinct — and instinct is what you're actually hiring.

points.title: Built for the US market
points.body: Over a decade operating inside American SaaS — not advising from a distance, but embedded and building. GTM dynamics, cultural context, how investors think at each stage: I understand this market from the inside, and I bring that directly into the work.

points.title: Close to the craft
points.body: I mentor product managers and speak at ProductCon LA, ProductWorld, Product School, and Product-Led Summit. Staying close to how good product thinking is built and how it spreads isn't a side project — it's part of how I keep what I bring to you sharp.

about.credentials: Kellogg MBA · Technion BS EE · IDF Major

items.q: What types of companies do you work with?
items.a: Growth-stage B2B SaaS companies — typically Series A to Series C — that are navigating a product inflection point. That might be scaling past product-market fit, entering a new market, rebuilding a team, or preparing for a raise.

items.q: How is this different from hiring a full-time CPO?
items.a: You get senior product leadership — strategy, execution, and team development — without the 12-month ramp, equity dilution, or full-time cost. Engagements are scoped to what you actually need, and can scale up or down as the business evolves.

items.q: What does a typical engagement look like?
items.a: Most engagements start with a focused strategy sprint — understanding the business, the product, and the gaps — then move into an ongoing operating rhythm: weekly leadership, prioritization, and cross-functional alignment. The exact shape depends on where you are and what you need.

items.q: Do you work with multiple companies simultaneously?
items.a: Yes, and deliberately so. Exposure to multiple growth-stage companies sharpens pattern recognition — what works, what doesn't, and how to move faster. I keep engagements limited to ensure each gets real attention.

items.q: How quickly can we get started?
items.a: Typically within 1–2 weeks. The first step is a 30-minute call to understand the situation and see if it's a good fit. If it is, we move fast.
Minimum three months. Real product leadership takes time to create durable results — a one-off advisory session is a different product. That said, strategy-only retainers can start shorter.

cta.label: Ready to find your inflection point?
cta.body: 15+ years of product leadership, on your team — without the full-time overhead.
